text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY - POST-OPERATIVE FOLLOW-UP AND TREATMENT PLANNING NOTE
Attending Oncologist: [Redacted]
Service: Gastrointestinal Medical Oncology

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Mr. Raymond Kowalski is a 59-year-old male with metastatic KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma presenting for post-operative oncology follow-up approximately 4 weeks after laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy. The primary purpose of today's visit is to assess surgical recovery, review final pathology from the resection specimen, discuss the trajectory of his metastatic disease, and plan transition to clinical trial enrollment for a KRAS G12D-targeted investigational protocol.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr. Kowalski is well known to this service. By way of summary, he is a 58-year-old male (now 59) who initially presented with a partially obstructing sigmoid colon mass and synchronous bilobar hepatic metastases, staged as cT4aN2bM1a (Stage IV) adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon. Biomarker profiling established KRAS exon 2, codon 12, p.G12D mutation; NRAS and BRAF wild-type; MMR-proficient/MSS; HER2 non-amplified; TMB low at 6.2 mut/Mb. CEA at diagnosis was 187.4 ng/mL, CA 19-9 was 412 U/mL. The KRAS G12D mutation definitively precluded anti-EGFR therapy.

He was treated with first-line mFOLFOX6 plus bev for 12 cycles, achieving a meaningful partial response in both hepatic and primary lesions (CEA nadir to approximately 28 ng/mL). Cumulative oxali-induced peripheral neuropathy (Grade 3 at worst) necessitated dose reduction and ultimately discontinuation of oxali after cycle 9/10. He transitioned to maintenance LV5FU2 plus Avastin per OPTIMOX strategy. During maintenance, he developed radiographic progression at approximately 10 months, with new bilateral pulmonary metastases and regrowth of hepatic lesions, accompanied by a sharp rise in CEA to 94.7 ng/mL. CT-guided biopsy of a right upper lobe pulmonary nodule confirmed metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma (CDX2+, CK20+, TTF-1 negative), ruling out a primary lung malignancy given his former smoking history.

He was transitioned to second-line FOLFIRI plus ramu. UGT1A1 genotyping showed *1/*28 heterozygosity (intermediate metabolizer), prompting a dose-attenuated start. He achieved an early partial response after 4 cycles (CEA to 61.3 ng/mL), subsequently maintaining stable disease through 8 cycles. However, iri dose reductions were required following a Grade 3 diarrhea/neutropenia hospitalization after cycle 6. Persistent treatment-related hypertension from Avastin and subsequently ramu has required escalating antihypertensive therapy (now on triple agents: lisinopril 20 mg, amlodipine 10 mg, hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg daily).

Approximately 16 months into his disease course, the multidisciplinary team and patient agreed to proceed with laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy for local control, given intermittent partial colonic obstruction, hematochezia, and the need for pre-trial surgical staging for the KRAS G12D inhibitor protocol. bev and ramu were held 4 weeks pre-operatively per standard anti-angiogenic surgery hold protocol. He underwent uncomplicated laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy with primary anastomosis approximately 4 weeks ago (estimated blood loss 120 mL, no intraoperative complications).

Today he returns for post-operative oncology evaluation. He reports overall good recovery. He is tolerating a regular diet without difficulty, bowel function has returned to baseline (1-2 formed stools per day), and he has resumed most activities of daily living. He notes persistent but manageable residual fatigue, which he attributes to the operation and the cumulative effects of approximately 16 months of systemic chemotherapy. He reports no fever, chills, wound complications, or symptoms of anastomotic leak. The residual peripheral neuropathy from prior oxali therapy is stable - described as mild tingling and numbness at the fingertips bilaterally, not interfering with daily activities at this point. He has resumed his antihypertensive medications and blood pressure has been running in the 138-148 systolic range at home on his log.

He is eager to initiate the KRAS G12D inhibitor clinical trial. He was pre-screened and found eligible prior to surgery: KRAS G12D-mutant CRC confirmed on both tumor tissue and ctDNA (VAF 3.8% at progression), ECOG 0-1, prior exposure to fluoropyrimidine and Eloxatin, no prior KRAS-targeted therapy.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
- Constitutional: Fatigue, moderate, improving week over week. No unintentional weight loss post-operatively. Appetite returning to normal.
- HEENT: No headache. No visual changes.
- Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations. No lower extremity edema.
- Respiratory: Mild exertional dyspnea on climbing two flights of stairs, similar to prior to surgery. No cough, no hemoptysis.
- GI: Bowel function normal post-surgery. No abdominal pain, no nausea, no hematochezia. Tolerating regular diet.
- GU: No hematuria, no dysuria. Urinary frequency unchanged.
- Neurologic: Residual fingertip paresthesias and numbness bilaterally (hands > feet), consistent with known residual oxali neuropathy. No new neurologic symptoms. No gait instability at this visit.
- Musculoskeletal: No new bone pain.
- Skin: Surgical incision sites healing well per patient report. No erythema or discharge.
- Psychiatric: Reports some anxiety about disease trajectory, otherwise mood appropriate. Continues to find purpose in planning to return to teaching in the fall.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY (Updated):
1. Metastatic KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma (Stage IV, synchronous bilobar hepatic metastases, subsequent pulmonary metastases), now s/p laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy
2. Hypertension - previously well-controlled on lisinopril, now requiring triple antihypertensive therapy (treatment-related component from VEGF/VEGFR inhibition)
3. Hyperlipidemia
4. Type 2 diabetes mellitus - well-controlled on metformin
5. Eloxatin-induced peripheral neuropathy (cumulative, residual Grade 1 at current evaluation)
6. VEGFR inhibitor-associated hypertension (Avastin then ramu)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
Former smoker, 20 pack-years, quit 8 years ago. Works as a high school history teacher (currently on medical leave). Married. Denies current alcohol use (limited to occasional social drinking prior to diagnosis, self-discontinued upon starting chemotherapy). No illicit drug use. Lives with spouse in a two-story home. Has completed advance directive (DPOA designated to spouse).

FAMILY HISTORY:
No family history of colorectal cancer. No known family history of hereditary cancer syndromes (Lynch syndrome, FAP). Parents deceased from cardiovascular disease.

ALLERGIES:
NKDA (no known drug allergies).

MEDICATIONS (Current, pre-trial):
1. Lisinopril 20 mg oral daily
2. Amlodipine 10 mg oral daily
3. Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg oral daily
4. Atorvastatin 20 mg oral daily
5. Metformin 1000 mg oral twice daily
6. Duloxetine 60 mg oral daily (for neuropathic pain management - residual)
7. Loperamide 2 mg as needed for diarrhea (limited use post-surgery)
8. Ondansetron 8 mg oral as needed for nausea
9. Multivitamin daily
(Avastin and ramu on hold since 4 weeks pre-operatively; FOLFIRI components also held post-operatively)

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vital Signs: Temperature 36.8°C, BP 142/92 mmHg (right arm, seated), HR 78 bpm (regular), RR 16/min, SpO2 97% on room air, Weight 79.3 kg, BMI 27.1 kg/m2.

General: Alert and oriented x3. Well-appearing male in no acute distress. Appears age-stated but mildly fatigued. Conversational, engaged.

HEENT: Normocephalic. Oropharynx clear, no mucositis. No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy appreciated on examination.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No peripheral edema bilaterally.

Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezing, rhonchi, or rales. Normal respiratory effort.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Well-healing laparoscopic port sites with no surrounding erythema, induration, or discharge (4 port sites, all healing appropriately per inspection). No palpable hepatomegaly. No palpable abdominal mass. Positive bowel sounds in all quadrants. No guarding or rigidity.

Extremities: No lower extremity edema. Peripheral pulses 2+ throughout.

Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Sensory exam: reduced light touch sensation in distal fingertips bilaterally (consistent with known residual neuropathy), bilateral feet with mild symmetric reduction in vibratory sensation. No gait ataxia on heel-toe walking. Motor strength 5/5 throughout upper and lower extremities.

Skin: Mild dry skin of hands and feet (residual from chemotherapy). No palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia. Surgical wounds healing without complication.

Lymph nodes: No palpable axillary, inguinal, or inguinal adenopathy.

ECOG Performance Status: 1 (restricted in physically strenuous activity but ambulatory and able to carry out work of a light or sedentary nature).

---

LABORATORY RESULTS:
CBC: WBC 6.8 x 10^9/L, ANC 4.2 x 10^9/L, Hgb 11.4 g/dL (mild anemia, improved from nadir), Hct 34.2%, Platelets 287 x 10^9/L.
CMP: Na 139, K 4.1, Cl 102, CO2 24, BUN 18, Creatinine 1.07 mg/dL, eGFR 74 mL/min/1.73m2 (stable), AST 28 U/L, ALT 31 U/L, Alk Phos 112 U/L, Total Bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL, Albumin 3.6 g/dL.
HbA1c (recent): 7.1% (diabetes well-controlled).
Coagulation: PT/INR 1.1.
Tumor Markers: CEA 48.2 ng/mL (down from 58.8 ng/mL at last check, likely reflecting partial contribution of primary tumor removal). CA 19-9: 162 U/mL (down from 189 U/mL).

---

IMAGING RESULTS:
Most recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (from approximately 2 months prior, after 8 cycles of FOLFIRI plus ramu) demonstrated stable disease: bilateral pulmonary nodules stable (right upper lobe 7 mm, right lower lobe 5 mm, left upper lobe 5 mm), hepatic lesions stable in segment VI (2.0 x 1.7 cm), segment II (0.9 x 0.8 cm), and segment IV (1.1 x 0.9 cm). No new lesions at that time. A repeat restaging CT is planned in approximately 4-6 weeks (after adequate surgical healing and prior to cycle 1 of clinical trial protocol) to establish a new baseline.

---

SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REVIEW (Laparoscopic Sigmoid Colectomy):
Final diagnosis: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of sigmoid colon, ypT3N1b, treatment response Grade 2 (partial histologic response - greater than 10% but less than 50% viable tumor cells remaining). This indicates moderate but incomplete response to prior systemic therapy. Proximal and distal margins negative. Circumferential radial margin negative (2.1 mm clearance). Lymph nodes: 2 of 13 positive for metastatic adenocarcinoma (deposits 0.4 cm and 0.6 cm). Lymphovascular invasion present. Perineural invasion present. AJCC 8th edition pathologic restaging: ypT3N1bM1b (remains Stage IVB by virtue of hepatic and pulmonary metastatic disease).

The presence of two positive mesenteric lymph nodes despite prior chemotherapy exposure, along with lymphovascular and perineural invasion, underscores the aggressive biology of this tumor and the importance of continued systemic therapy. The partial histologic response (Grade 2) is consistent with the radiographic partial response observed during mFOLFOX6 induction.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

1. METASTATIC KRAS G12D-MUTANT, MSS SIGMOID COLON ADENOCARCINOMA (Stage IVB, ypT3N1bM1b), s/p laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy:

Mr. Kowalski has now completed surgical resection of his primary tumor and is approximately 4 weeks post-op with excellent recovery. His metastatic disease burden includes stable bilobar hepatic lesions (three measurable lesions in segments II, IV, VI) and three bilateral pulmonary nodules that have remained stable on FOLFIRI plus ramu. His CEA has declined modestly to 48.2 ng/mL, which likely reflects in part the removal of the primary tumor as a contributor to marker production, and also ongoing disease suppression.

His overall disease trajectory is that of a patient who has achieved meaningful but not complete disease control on two lines of therapy, now 18 months from diagnosis. For KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS metastatic CRC, this is consistent with the expected pattern - median overall survival in the 18-24 month range historically, though outcomes continue to improve with access to novel targeted approaches.

Plan: Transition to the KRAS G12D inhibitor plus Mektovi combination clinical trial protocol. Patient met all enrollment criteria prior to surgery (KRAS G12D-mutant CRC confirmed on tissue and ctDNA, ECOG 0-1, prior fluoropyrimidine and oxali exposure, no prior KRAS-targeted therapy, adequate organ function). Trial registration and IRB paperwork completed. Target start date approximately 2-4 weeks from today (approximately 6 weeks post-op total, allowing adequate surgical healing per protocol requirements). Prior to trial initiation, will obtain:
   - Repeat CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (new baseline imaging)
   - Repeat CBC, CMP, LFTs, CEA, CA 19-9 (new baseline labs)
   - EKG (protocol requirement for QTc monitoring given Mektovi)
   - Ophthalmology clearance (bini-associated retinal toxicity screening)
   - Endocrine check (fasting glucose and HbA1c - MEK inhibitors can affect glucose metabolism, relevant given his T2DM)

2. TREATMENT-RELATED HYPERTENSION (Grade 2, attributed to prior bev and Cyramza):

Current BP 142/92 mmHg on triple antihypertensive therapy (lisinopril 20 mg, amlodipine 10 mg, HCTZ 25 mg daily). Blood pressure improved since holding anti-VEGF/VEGFR agents in the peri-operative period. Nephrology is co-managing. Will monitor blood pressure closely once systemic therapy resumes (the investigational protocol does not include Avastin or ramu, which may allow for gradual antihypertensive de-escalation over time if BP remains controlled). Target BP < 140/90 mmHg. Cardiology referral considered if unable to achieve adequate control on current regimen once systemic therapy resumes.

3. Eloxatin-INDUCED PERIPHERAL NEUROPATHY (Residual Grade 1):

Continued improvement noted since discontinuing oxali. Now Grade 1 (mild distal fingertip paresthesias, not functionally limiting). Duloxetine 60 mg daily continued for neuropathic symptom management. Prognosis for further recovery is guarded given total cumulative Eloxatin exposure received; partial recovery expected but complete resolution unlikely. Patient has adapted well and is not reporting functional limitation.

4. TYPE 2 DIABETES MELLITUS:

HbA1c 7.1% - well controlled on metformin 1000 mg twice daily. Metformin held perioperatively and resumed. Of note, MEK inhibitors (Mektovi in the trial) can be associated with hyperglycemia; will monitor fasting glucose closely during trial protocol. Endocrinology awareness flagged.

5. MILD ANEMIA:

Hgb 11.4 g/dL, consistent with anemia of chronic disease and post-operative recovery. Iron studies pending from today's labs. Will re-check in 4-6 weeks. Transfusion not indicated at this time given no symptoms of anemia (no exertional chest pain, no lightheadedness). If iron-deficient component identified, will initiate oral iron supplementation or IV iron pending tolerance.

6. GOALS OF CARE / PSYCHOSOCIAL:

Extensive discussion held today with Mr. Kowalski and his wife regarding the overall disease trajectory. At 18 months from a Stage IV colorectal cancer diagnosis with KRAS G12D mutation, he remains in good functional status (ECOG 1) and is motivated to pursue additional treatment. He understands the disease is not curable with current standard therapies and that the goal of treatment remains disease control and quality of life preservation. He has completed his advance directive with his wife designated as healthcare proxy. He expressed cautious optimism about the KRAS G12D trial and understands it is investigational.

He is planning to return to part-time teaching in the fall if his energy permits, which we strongly encourage as a meaningful quality-of-life activity. He will continue to work with our social work team and has been connected with a colorectal cancer support group. Nutrition consultation has been requested given mild weight loss and post-operative nutritional needs.

Return to clinic: 2-3 weeks for pre-trial screening visit. Trial pharmacy and research nurse will coordinate prior to that visit for study drug ordering and protocol orientation.

Electronically signed by: [Attending Medical Oncologist, GI Oncology]
Dictated and reviewed by: [Redacted]
CC: Primary Care Physician, Colorectal Surgery (Dr. [Redacted]), Nephrology, Clinical Trial Coordinator
